title: ononis pendula subsp. boissieri
intro: Ononis pendula subsp. boissieri, also known as Boissier's gromwell, is a perennial plant in the legume family (Fabaceae), specifically a subspecies of the Ononis pendula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving gastrointestinal issues due to its ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea. Herbal preparations of Ononis pendula subsp. boissieri include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health.
